column8: Leon has known his friends for a year. He can talk with them about everything and they rarely disagree____________one another.
column9: One day, however, he and his friend Jarrod got into terrible argument over sport. Leon was quite surprised, actually, because the argument got so bad that Jarrod_______________out of his house. Leon had never been so upset in his life.
column10: A week went by and they hadn't spoken to one another. Leon told his other friends what had happened, but they didn't seen Jarrod in a while. Leon guessed he had to be the one to__________out to him.
column11: He decided to visit Jarrod's house and meet him face-toface. He knew they had to speak eventually and he didn't want to__________________the agony. But no one answered the door when he rang.
column12: Confused, Leon thought he would just leave a note. "I'm sorry we got into argument the other day. Please, let's talk soon," he wrote. Later that night Leon received a call from Jarrod. Strangely, Jarrod tried to_____________that it wasn't a big deal.
column13: "Sorry, I guess I just let my emotions get the_______________of me. It was really stupid. Forgive me," said Jarrod.
column14: That was all Leon needed to hear, and the two of them laid the incident to_________ .
